reasoning: Zootopia 2 releases on November 26, 2025, which gives it exactly 35 days to accumulate "calendar gross" before the December 31 cutoff. Compounding this structural disadvantage is the release of 'Avatar: Fire and Ash' on December 19, which will monopolize premium screens and dominate the lucrative Christmas box office window, stunting Zootopia 2's late holiday legs. Furthermore, Zootopia 2 has to outgross the calendar-year totals of major summer tentpoles—like Superman, Jurassic World Rebirth, and Fantastic Four—which have their entire theatrical runs (100% of their box office) falling within 2025. Historically, late-November animated releases (even massive hits like 'Frozen II' and 'Moana 2') have never secured the #1 calendar year spot because they simply run out of days, and there's almost always a summer or spring release that crosses the $400M-$600M+ mark. Given these severe time and competition constraints, the probability of Zootopia 2 securing the #1 domestic calendar gross in 2025 is exceedingly low.
